## Intro to Issues

In this section of the document I hope to provide broad reference information as well as a synopsis of waht I think of as core issues for the use of water in the context of the irrigation district and our property. 

### Water Rights

This may become it's own section but it is certainly important to recognize that the whole issue of water rights themselves is fraught with history and conflict. This issue is also concerned with where the water comes from and systems that are designed and operated alongside the natural flow of water.

### Water Delivery

This is about how water gets delivered to the user. Simplistically this is about the irrigation system and how the water gets from where it is stored or originates and arrives at the end user. We generally talk about main canals, laterals which are owned and operated by the irrigation district as well as the private laterals which are the privately developed and maintained ditches that move water across private property and are NOT owned and operated by the irrigation district.

### Water Use

Historically agricultural water in Central Oregon has been applied to the ground through flood irrigation. This is essentially a process of applying enough water to the ground at a high enough rate that gravity can move it across the ground being irrigated. Sprinkler irrigation is growing across the region because it's more efficient but creates expense for the user both through capital investment and electrical costs for pumping.
